name: Claroty
description: Broad cyber-physical systems protection platform covering OT, IoT, and healthcare environments. Passive visibility, continuous threat detection, and vulnerability management. Offers both on-premises (CTD) and cloud-based (xDome) deployment.
best_fit: Large enterprises needing visibility across mixed OT, IoT, and IT environments. Strong in manufacturing, healthcare, and critical infrastructure.
watch: Product consolidation between CTD and xDome roadmap ongoing.

name: Dragos
description: OT threat intelligence-first platform built by ICS security practitioners. Asset visibility, risk-based vulnerability management, and threat detection with contextualized response playbooks. Deep ICS protocol coverage and a managed services arm.
best_fit: Organizations building mature, threat intelligence-driven OT security programs. Strong in energy, utilities, oil and gas, and manufacturing.

name: Nozomi Networks
description: AI-driven asset intelligence and anomaly detection with strong distributed visibility across large OT and IoT environments. Guardian sensors aggregate into the cloud-based Vantage platform. Includes wireless spectrum monitoring.
best_fit: Large, multi-site industrial operations — utilities, distributed energy, transportation — needing consistent OT and IoT visibility at scale.

name: Industrial Defender
description: OT asset management and compliance-focused visibility platform with deep NERC CIP alignment. Automates evidence collection and reporting for CIP compliance alongside continuous OT network monitoring.
best_fit: Bulk electric system operators with NERC CIP compliance requirements and limited internal OT security staff.

name: OTORIO
description: OT risk management platform combining asset visibility with business-context risk scoring. Prioritizes remediation based on operational impact rather than raw vulnerability severity.
best_fit: Mid-size manufacturers and industrial operators that need risk prioritization without the overhead of an enterprise platform.

name: Radiflow
description: Unified OT visibility, risk management, and incident response platform (Radiflow360) built for mid-market industrial enterprises. AI-enhanced threat assessment and compliance streamlining.
best_fit: Mid-size industrial enterprises needing a unified OT security platform without enterprise-scale complexity or cost.
watch: Doubling sales in 2025 — watch for upmarket move.

name: Rhebo
description: OT network monitoring and anomaly detection focused on energy utilities and industrial automation. Deep protocol coverage for IEC 61850, IEC 60870, and GOOSE — protocols common in grid environments that most platforms cover poorly.
best_fit: Energy utilities and grid operators needing OT visibility with strong coverage of energy-specific protocols.

name: Verve Industrial
description: OT endpoint visibility and vulnerability management with active (agent-based) and passive options. Focuses on remediating vulnerabilities in legacy OT assets — patching, configuration hardening, and change management.
best_fit: Organizations ready to move beyond passive detection toward active remediation of OT endpoint vulnerabilities.

name: Armis
description: Agentless asset intelligence platform covering IT, OT, IoT, and medical devices in a unified view. Strong integrations with major SIEM, SOAR, and CMDB platforms.
best_fit: Enterprises where the CISO owns OT security and needs a single asset risk view across IT and OT without separate tooling.

name: Cisco IoT Security
description: OT and IoT visibility integrated into Cisco's broader security and networking portfolio. Leverages existing Cisco network infrastructure for passive OT asset discovery.
best_fit: Organizations already heavily invested in Cisco networking infrastructure wanting OT visibility without adding a standalone platform.

name: Microsoft Defender for IoT
description: Agentless OT and IoT asset discovery and threat detection integrated into the Microsoft security stack. Native integration with Microsoft Sentinel and Defender XDR.
best_fit: Organizations running Microsoft-centric security stacks wanting OT visibility without adding a separate vendor relationship.
watch: OT protocol coverage still narrower than pure-play platforms. Check annually.

name: Tenable OT Security
description: OT asset visibility and vulnerability management that integrates with Tenable's broader vulnerability management portfolio. Bridges OT and IT vulnerability data in a unified risk view.
best_fit: Organizations already running Tenable for IT vulnerability management wanting to extend coverage to OT environments.

name: Darktrace (OT)
description: AI-based anomaly detection extending from IT into OT environments via its Industrial module. Uses unsupervised machine learning to establish behavioral baselines across converged environments.
best_fit: Organizations already running Darktrace for IT security wanting to extend its AI detection model into OT without a separate tool.
strengths: No separate vendor relationship if already a Darktrace customer
watch: Acquisition target. Small OT customer base limits product improvement feedback loop. OT module is thin vs pure-play platforms.

name: Stellar Cyber
description: Open XDR platform with OT asset visibility and threat detection alongside IT coverage. Ingests data from OT sensors and correlates across the full IT/OT environment.
best_fit: Organizations building a unified SOC across IT and OT that want to consolidate tooling rather than run separate platforms.
watch: Watch for funding rounds or pivot away from OT focus.

name: Fortinet (OT)
description: Ruggedized industrial firewall and network security appliances for harsh OT environments. FortiGate Rugged series handles temperature extremes. Integrates with Fortinet Security Fabric.
best_fit: Organizations needing ruggedized perimeter enforcement in industrial environments, particularly manufacturing and energy.

name: Palo Alto Networks (OT)
description: OT security extending Palo Alto's NGFW capabilities into industrial environments. Passive asset discovery and threat prevention inline. Ruggedized hardware options available.
best_fit: Organizations already running Palo Alto NGFWs wanting to extend enforcement capabilities into OT without a separate vendor.

name: TXOne Networks
description: OT-native endpoint and network protection purpose-built for ICS environments. Portable inspection tools for air-gapped environments and endpoint agents for legacy OT assets. No internet connectivity required.
best_fit: Organizations needing OT endpoint protection for legacy assets in air-gapped or semi-isolated environments.

name: Elisity
description: Identity-based microsegmentation enforcing least-privilege access across OT environments without network redesign. Policy enforcement happens at the software layer over existing infrastructure.
best_fit: Organizations that need network segmentation for compliance or risk reduction but cannot tolerate a physical network redesign.
watch: Watch for reference customer growth and competitive response from established vendors.

name: PacketViper
description: OT network deception and active defense platform. Deploys decoy assets within OT networks to detect and deflect attackers before they reach real systems.
best_fit: Organizations with a visibility platform already in place looking to add an active deception layer.
watch: Smaller challenger — confirm actively maintained before recommending.

name: Honeywell Forge
description: OT cybersecurity services and software from an industrial automation incumbent. Forge Cybersecurity Suite covers asset visibility, risk assessment, and managed detection.
best_fit: Process industry operators — oil and gas, chemicals, refining — already running Honeywell automation systems.

name: Rockwell Automation
description: OT security services and FactoryTalk network security tools. Cybersecurity assessments, network design, and managed services primarily for Rockwell-centric manufacturing environments.
best_fit: Discrete and hybrid manufacturing organizations running Allen-Bradley and FactoryTalk automation infrastructure.

name: Cyolo
description: Zero-trust secure remote access built around OT constraints. Identity-based access with continuous session validation. Designed to replace insecure remote access practices without disrupting operations.
best_fit: Organizations replacing legacy VPN-based remote access for OT environments, or managing third-party vendor access.

name: OPSWAT
description: OT endpoint and media security for removable media and file transfers into air-gapped OT environments. MetaDefender Kiosk scans USB drives before they enter secure OT zones.
best_fit: Organizations with air-gapped OT environments where removable media is a primary attack vector.

name: Phosphorus
description: Proactive security for IoT and OT devices — firmware updates, credential management, and configuration hardening at scale across large device populations.
best_fit: Organizations with large IoT and OT device populations needing automated remediation rather than passive vulnerability detection.
watch: Emerging category — watch for consolidation or commoditization.

name: Xage Security
description: Zero trust access and identity platform for OT and critical infrastructure. Distributed enforcement fabric works without central infrastructure — nodes enforce policy independently.
best_fit: Critical infrastructure operators needing zero trust access enforcement across distributed, remote, or air-gapped OT environments.
